business.headline: Why Indians can't access Rs 2 lakh crore in unclaimed assets, even if it's theirsUnclaimed assets across bank deposits, shares, insurance policies and retirement funds have crossed Rs 2 lakh crore. This article explains why this money remains out of reach and what makes it so hard to recover.

Buying a car or bike? The hidden truth behind insurance sold at dealershipsWhen you buy a car or bike, the final bill usually comes as an 'on-road price' that includes insurance. For most buyers, this is accepted without much thought, as it appears to be a standard part of the purchase. But what often goes unnoticed is that this insurance is not mandatory to buy from the dealer.

Middle East war to cut global growth, spike inflation despite truce: World BankThe World Bank's baseline estimate now projects growth in emerging markets and developing economies of 3.65 per cent in 2026, compared to 4 per cent in October, dropping as low as 2.6 per cent in an adverse scenario with a longer-lasting war.

India allows 4 sanctioned tankers carrying Iranian oil to berth at Sikka: ReportIranian oil is often transported by a so-called shadow fleet of vessels that lack internationally recognised insurance and safety certifications. But this requires special permission from the government as exemptions are required under Indian rules for the berthing of ships.

Gold dips, silver slips: Is this the right time to invest or wait?Prices of gold and silver have cooled off after recent highs, but the bigger question remains, is this the right entry point, or is there more downside ahead?

Why April is the best time to reset your budget, SIPs and other investmentsIn Part 2 of our MONEY MAKEOVER series, we explore how April, with salary hikes, bonuses and tax changes, creates the perfect window to pause, reset and move forward with clarity. Let's have a look.

Necessary evolution: SP Group backs Tata Sons listing, seeks RBI clarityIn a statement, Shapoorji Pallonji Mistry said a timely listing is not merely about regulatory compliance, but a step that would reinforce the foundational principles of the Tata Group.

Global shocks may hit every 1–2 years, warns Axis Bank's chief economistAxis Bank chief economist Neelkanth Mishra warns that geopolitical shocks could hit every 1–2 years and explains what India must do to prepare its economy.

Equity inflows jump 55% in March, but debt outflows drag industry negative: AMFIThe data clearly shows that retail investors are becoming more confident and disciplined. While institutional money continues to move in and out of debt funds based on short-term needs, individual investors are steadily increasing their exposure to equities.
Dalal Street records best week in 5 years on Iran-US ceasefire reliefSensex closes 919 points higher, Nifty crosses 24,000; IT stocks tumble

Unclaimed EPF rises to Rs 10,900 crore: Why it's rising and how to claim your moneyYou may not think much about your old PF account after switching jobs, but it could still be holding your money. Unclaimed EPF funds have now crossed Rs 10,900 crore, raising concerns about how easily savings are getting left behind.

TCS shifts focus to AI, infrastructure as demand outlook remains mixedShares of TCS were trading at Rs 2,513 on the BSE, down 2.89% or Rs 74.75, as of 1:27 pm, even as its fourth-quarter performance beat estimates.

RBI wants a 1-hour pause on some UPI payments. Here's whyThe Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released a discussion paper proposing a one-hour delay for certain digital payments. Here's an explainer on how it could work, which transactions it may affect, and what it means for users.

Sending over Rs 10,000 via UPI? RBI may soon introduce a 1-hour delayIf you often use UPI for bigger payments, a change may be on the way. The RBI is looking at adding a one-hour pause for transfers above Rs 10,000 to help prevent online fraud.

Applying for TAN? Income Tax Dept replaces Form 49B with two new formsApplying for a TAN is set to become a lot simpler. The Income Tax Department has replaced the old Form 49B with two new forms, aiming to make the process clearer and easier for different types of applicants. Let's have a look.

Infosys, TCS, HCLTech: Why IT stocks are down despite market rallyThe fall in IT stocks comes even as the Sensex and Nifty moved higher, showing that the weakness is limited to the sector amid concerns around growth and demand.

Sun Pharma shares fall over 4% amid acquisition concernsSun Pharma shares fell over 3.5%, as investor concerns over a potential mega acquisition outweighed optimism, bringing focus on debt, integration risks and the deal's long-term payoff.

TCS shares down 3%: Why the IT giant's stock fell despite strong Q4 result?TCS shares down: The stock was trading at around Rs 2,515.00, down 2.81% as of 10:15 am. It was also among the top losers on both the Nifty50 and the IT index.

Wipro shares rise as board considers buyback after three yearsWipro has informed exchanges that its board will evaluate a share buyback alongside its fourth-quarter results on April 16, bringing the focus back on shareholder returns.
Sensex ends 900 points higher: 3 reasons why stock market rose todayThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 687.99 points to 77,319.64, while the NSE Nifty50 added 211.60 points to 23,986.70 as of 9:58 am.

entertainment.headline: Ranveer Singh vs Ranbir Kapoor: Who is Bollywood's real shape-shifter? The Ranveer Singh-Ranbir Kapoor debate is back, sparked by Dhurandhar: The Revenge and Ramayana Part I. But this time, it goes beyond stardom - into two radically different ideas of what it means to transform on screen.

Deeply concerned: PM Modi prays for Asha Bhosle's recovery amid health scare Prime Minister Narendra Modi said he was deeply concerned about Asha Bhosle's health after her hospitalisation in Mumbai. The 92-year-old singer is under treatment after suffering from extreme exhaustion and chest infection.

Ranveer Allahbadia reacts to Samay Raina's jabs on Still Alive: Kaun Samay? Podcaster Ranveer Allahbadia reacted to comedian Samay Raina's jokes in his comedy special Still Alive. His remarks come amid renewed chatter around the India's Got Latent controversy.

Viral: Shah Rukh Khan grooves at Anant Ambani's birthday bhajan sandhya Shah Rukh Khan's dance from Anant Ambani's Bhajan Sandhya in Jamnagar has taken over social media. Videos from the night show a host of celebrities, including Ranveer Singh and Janhvi Kapoor, dancing at the event.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 24: No competition for Ranveer Singh's film yet Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge continues its steady box office run on Day 24 across India and overseas. The Ranveer Singh-starrer is edging towards becoming one of the top 3 Indian all-time grossers.

Euphoria 3 star Zendaya recalls draining war zone scene that left her bruised Zendaya opened up on the draining war zone scene in Euphoria that left her bruised. The third season of the series also features actors Sydney Sweeney and Jacob Elordi among others.

Jana Nayagan leak claims baseless; CBFC explains film's security protocols The CBFC (Central Board of Film Certification) rejected claims about Vijay's Jana Nayagan leak as baseless. Issuing a formal clarification, the board highlighted its stringent security protocols.

Hopefully everything will be well: Asha Bhosle's granddaughter Zanai Veteran singer Asha Bhosle, 92, has been hospitalised due to extreme exhaustion and a chest infection, according to a health update shared by her granddaughter Zanai Bhosle on social media.

Asha Bhosle admitted to Breach Candy Hospital; granddaughter Zanai shares update Playback legend Asha Bhosle was admitted to Breach Candy Hospital, Mumbai, on Saturday night. Her granddaughter Zanai posted on social media that the 92-year-old singer suffered from extreme exhaustion and a chest infection, and requested privacy.

Viral: Dhurandhar's Madhavan, Arjun Rampal hope Pak hosts Indian awards in old video An old clip, featuring R Madhavan and Arjun Rampal among other Bollywood stars, shows them hoping Pakistan gets to host an Indian awards show. The video has gone viral amid the success of Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

It's Shoe-time: Watch Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, KJo at Devil Wears Prada 2 event Meryl Streep reviewed Karan Johar's footwear during the promotional tour for The Devil Wears Prada 2 in Tokyo. The light-hearted exchange saw Johar foot a query or two from Anne Hathaway, too.

RGV says he never got threats from the underworld: They loved Satya, Company Ram Gopal Varma recalled the underworld's impact on his iconic films, Satya and Company. In turn, he added, the underworld, too, loved his films.

Outcome review: Keanu Reeves shines in black comedy about flaws, self-acceptanceRelease Date:Fri Apr 10, 2026 Outcome review: Jonah Hill's black comedy-drama offers a subdued look at celebrity guilt and human imperfections. The film is elevated by strong performances yet limited by familiar storytelling that doesn't quite linger.

Happy birthday my momo: Saiyaara's Aneet Padda wishes Mohit Suri with a wild video Aneet Padda marked filmmaker Mohit Suri's birthday with a heartfelt Instagram post. The Saiyaara actor also shared a wonky video, adding a dash of humour to her gratitude for the director.

DDLJ in Academy of Motion Pictures list of top travel romance films. Kajol reacts Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge has been included in The Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences' list of top romance films that involve travel. Check out how Kajol reacted to the post.

Supriya Ganesh, Maitreya Ramakrishnan slam troll's colourist, 'kamwali bai' jibe Actors Supriya Ganesh and Maitreyi Ramakrishnan hit back at a troll targeting Indian representation in Hollywood. They called out the stereotypes highlighted in a post, also involving Charithra Chandran and Shabana Azeez.

Had a calling: Sara Arjun attends Bhasma Aarti in Ujjain amid Dhurandhar 2 success As Dhurandhar 2 continues to break records at the box office, actor Sara Arjun visited the Mahakaleshwar Temple in Ujjain on Saturday. She attended the Bhasma Aarti and called the experience overwhelming.

Jana Nayagan actor Pooja Hegde hurt by leak: Watch Vijay's last film in theatres Jana Nayagan actor Pooja Hegde reacted to the film's leak by calling it disheartening and a blow to the efforts of the entire team. Urging fans to avoid piracy, she appealed to audiences to watch Vijay's final film the right way.

Ben Affleck gives Jennifer Lopez his share of $60M Beverly Hills home: Report Ben Affleck and Jennifer Lopez's divorce proceedings took a new turn with a fresh update about their Beverly Hills home. The development comes nearly a year after Lopez spoke about the emotional toll of their split.

general news.headline: We're returning without a deal: JD Vance on truce talks with Iran in Islamabad Live UpdatesWashington and Tehran failed to reach a final consensus over the prevailing Middle East conflict in Islamabad as Iran did not agree to the terms offered by the United States, US Vice President JD Vance said.

A BIG NO: Iran says talks failed as US demanded all it couldn't achieve from warIran's reaction came soon after US Vice President JD Vance left Islamabad after talks faltered as Washington and Tehran failed to reach a final consensus over the Middle East war that lasted for nearly 40 days before a temporary two-week ceasefire was announced.

Trump warns China of 'big problems' if it provides air defence systems to IranDonald Trump warned China would face "big problems" if it sends weapons to Iran, after US intelligence suggested Beijing may secretly route air defence systems through third countries. China denied the allegation, calling it baseless.

This is the last warning: US warships' secret foray caused tense Hormuz standoffTwo US Navy destroyers passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday, prompting a challenge from Iranian forces, even as high-level talks between Washington and Tehran were underway in Islamabad.

The mystery of missing LPG cylinders: Delivered, but not receivedTalk about the massive raids to recover LPG cylinders from the black market in India. Have you wondered how these make it there? We think we stumbled upon one of the ways in which dealers scam the system: ghost deliveries.

Phase 2 of SIR ends with over 7.2 crore deletions across 12 states, UP topsAt the start of the SIR exercise, the combined electorate in these states and UT stood at around 51 crore. The deletions account for roughly 10.2 per cent of the voter base.

The art of being savage ft. IranWhile missiles and drones fall from the skies, Iran is still busy posting memes and managing its image for the world. Because in the modern world, war is no longer just fought on battlefields. It is fought on timelines, in captions, and in who tells the story best. There are no clear winners in the US-Israel-Iran war on the battlefield yet, but Iran is clearly winning the propaganda war. It is projecting itself not as a victim but as fearless, strong, and unapologetically savage.

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

I will quickly learn Spanish. It won't take too long. I'm good at language and I will go to Venezuela. I'm going to run for president." President Donald J. Trump, April 5, 2026, Washington D.C.

Watch: Artemis 2 crew members get emotional, hug during Nasa eventThe Artemis II capsule and its four-member crew streaked through Earth's atmosphere and safely splashed down in the Pacific Ocean on April 10 after nearly 10 days in space, capping the first voyage by humans to the vicinity of the moon in over half a century.

Kunal Kamra invokes Balasaheb's political cartoons to defend free speechKamra argued that Thackeray spent decades criticising and ridiculing authority figures, including Prime Ministers, without ever facing breach of privilege actions. He reminded the committee that political satire forms the foundation of the ideology Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde claims to represent.

Live: Pakistan urges US, Iran to uphold ceasefire as talks failUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Talks between Iran and the US faltered on Sunday after US Vice President JD Vance said that Tehran did not accept US's terms to end the war. Vance has reportedly left for the US following the talks hitting a roadblock. The first phase of US-Iran talks in Islamabad concluded with both sides exchanging written texts to confirm agreements, even as earlier face-to-face meetings marked the start of direct negotiations. Limited progress was reported on key issues, including possible restrictions on strikes in southern Lebanon and movement on unfreezing Iranian assets. Iran has maintained hard red lines, insisting on a Lebanon ceasefire and broader conditions, while tensions remain high with US mine-clearing operations underway in the Strait of Hormuz and warnings issued to American vessels.

Actor Kashika Kapoor never ignores this one symptom and you shouldn't eitherIn this series, Kashika Kapoor speaks about her approach to health, the symptoms she pays attention to, the habits she considers non-negotiable, and the personal practices she follows for a healthy mind and body.

sports.headline: Gout Gout shatters Usain Bolt's record, runs 200m sprint in 19.67 seconds Gout Gout clocked 19.67 seconds to win the 200m at the Australian Championships, becoming the first Australian to break the 20-second barrier. He has also shattered Usain Bolt's U23 timing by a big margin.

Nitish Rana fined after heated spat with umpires during CSK vs DC clash IPL 2026: Nitish Rana's frustration in a high-pressure moment ended up costing him more than just momentum in the game. While the controversy centred around a glove change, it was his reaction that ultimately drew disciplinary action from the IPL.

Kerala rejoices: Shashi Tharoor praises Sanju Samson after maiden CSK century IPL 2026: Sanju Samson's breakthrough knock for CSK didn't just win a match, it lit up Kerala as well. Backed for years by Shashi Tharoor, the century felt like a moment of validation as much as a return to form.

16 meetings, 1651 points each: Alcaraz meets Sinner in a tennis final once again Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner meet in the Monte Carlo Masters final with the world No. 1 ranking on the line. Across 16 meetings, the two have won 1651 points each, underlining how little separates them.

Samson's calmness near Dhoni level: CSK's confidence on star opener pays off IPL 2026: Sanju Samson's first big knock for CSK could not have come at a better time, both for him and the team. Backed by CSK bowling coach Eric Simmons, his calm, composed century might just be the turning point this season.

Ayush Shetty proved that badminton should not be 15-point game: Coach Badminton Asia Championships: Ayush Shetty's incredible win against World No.1 Kunlavut Vitidsarn proves why badminton should not be reduced to a 15-point game. The Badminton World Federation wants to shorten the match durations in the hopes of making the sport more broadcast friendly.

IPL Play Of The Day: Not Samson, Delhi's butter fingers prove costly in Chennai IPL 2026: On a day when fielding proved to be the difference, Delhi Capitals handled the ball like a bar of soap, which ultimately cost them the match against Chennai Super Kings at the Chepauk stadium.

Rajasthan manager caught using phone in dugout, Lalit Modi flags IPL rule breach IPL 2026: A viral clip showed Rajasthan Royals manager Romi Bhinder using a phone in the dugout during their game against Royal Challengers Bengaluru on Friday. The moment, flagged by Lalit Modi, has sparked questions over a potential breach of IPL anti-corruption rules.

Team first: Ayush Mhatre explains CSK call to retire him out for Shivam Dube Ayush Mhatre explains CSK's call to retire him out for Shivam Dube, saying team comes first as the move helps Chennai Super Kings post 212 and secure their first win of IPL 2026.

Samson's Padayappa celebration: CSK star reveals special dedication after DC win Sanju Samson revealed the reason behind his Padyappa celebration and who it was dedicated to after scoring his maiden hundred for CSK against DC at the Chepauk on Saturday, April 11.

R Vaishali stuns Goryachkina to take sole lead in CandidatesR Vaishali defeated Aleksandra Goryachkina in Round 11 to take sole lead in the Women's Candidates, extending her advantage as rivals Anna Muzychuk and Jiner Zhu dropped points.

Tristan Stubbs lashes out after glove change denial proves costly in ChennaiTristan Stubbs was left frustrated after being denied a glove change in humid Chennai, a moment that disrupted his rhythm and proved costly as Delhi Capitals fell short against CSK.

Ton of relief: Sanju Samson arrives in yellow as CSK beat DC to end home curseAfter a string of low-key outings, Sanju Samson returned to form in emphatic fashion, smashing a superb hundred as Chennai Super Kings, backed by a sharp effort from their bowlers, outclassed Delhi Capitals by 23 runs to register their first win of the Indian Premier League 2026 on Saturday.

Sooryavanshi will make people forget Abhishek: Ex-Pak cricketer's bizarre claimTanveer Ahmed's bold claim on Vaibhav Suryavanshi eclipsing Abhishek Sharma sparks debate as the 15-year-old continues his stunning IPL 2026 rise with fearless batting displays.

Ayush Shetty chases history. When and where to watch Asia Championship final liveAyush Shetty, 20, will take on World No. 2 Shi Yu Qi of China on Sunday, bidding to become the first Indian man since Dinesh Khanna in 1965 to win the men's singles title at Badminton Asia Championships. The World No. 25 has been on a giant-slaying run in Ningbo, China.

Archery World Cup: Compound women salvage pride with team gold after recurve flopThe Indian compound women's trio of Jyothi Surekha Vennam, Madhura Dhamangaonkar and Pragati edged past the USA 233-232 to clinch the gold medal at the Archery World Cup Stage 1 here on Saturday.

Sarfaraz's blinder sends Chepauk in frenzy, Gurjapneet takes wicket on IPL debutSarfaraz Khan's stunning catch handed Gurjapneet Singh a wicket on his first IPL ball, derailing Delhi Capitals' chase and turning momentum firmly in Chennai Super Kings' favour.

Jasprit Bumrah's drought no concern for MI, Pollard says defence matters tooJasprit Bumrah's quiet start hasn't rattled MI, with Kieron Pollard backing his impact beyond wickets ahead of the RCB clash at Wankhede.

Ayush Mhatre becomes 2nd CSK player to be retired out after scoring fiftyCSK batter Ayush Mhatre was retired out on 59 off 36 balls against Delhi Capitals at the MA Chidambaram Stadium on Saturday night, after bringing up his second half-century of the season.

Sanju Samson celebrates his first CSK hundred with Rajnikanth's Padayappa saluteSanju Samson has made a sensational comeback to form as he scored his maiden IPL hundred for Chennai Super Kings during the clash against Delhi Capitals on Saturday, April 11.

Ishan Kishan laments SRH's poor finish with the bat in six-wicket loss to PBKSIshan Kishan admitted that Sunrisers Hyderabad fell well short of a potential 250 total despite a flying start, pointing to poor execution with the bat in the death overs. Punjab Kings, led by Shreyas Iyer, capitalised on that lapse with a composed chase to seal a six-wicket win.

Advantage Manchester City? Arsenal stumble in title race with Bournemouth lossAlex Scott's decisive strike halts Arsenal's momentum and tightens the title race as the Gunners lose 1-2 to Bournemouth at the Emirates Stadium. This means that the advantage is with Manchester City, who have two games in hand over Arsenal.

Animesh Kujur overcomes false start setback, shines with 100m-200m doubleStar sprinter Animesh Kujur of Odisha dominated the Indian Athletics Series one-day meet, winning both the 100m and 200m events in a competitive outing on Saturday.

IPL 2026: PBKS gun down 220 to crush SRH despite Abhishek Sharma's heroicsPunjab Kings outclassed Sunrisers Hyderabad in a battle of explosive top orders, chasing down 220 with authority in Mullanpur to seal a six-wicket win in IPL 2026 and spoil what had begun as Abhishek Sharma's night of dominance.

Auqib Nabi finally makes IPL debut for DC, Dewald Brevis returns for CSKAuqib Nabi finally gets the chance to make his IPL debut for DC as he is part of the lineup to face off against CSK at the Chepauk on Saturday, April 11.

LSG assistant coach Lance Klusener credits bowling for good IPL startLucknow Super Giants assistant coach Lance Klusener believes the team's overseas batters are "sleeping giants" who will peak at the right time, while crediting the bowling unit for driving their recent wins. Strong contributions from the Indian batting and bowling core have kept LSG competitive, even as their overseas stars are yet to hit top gear.

Watch: Athletics Federation under fire after man walks onto track during raceA man walking onto the track during a junior 200m race in New Delhi has sparked outrage, with fans slamming the Athletics Federation of India over safety lapses and poor event management.

CSK vs DC Highlights: Chennai open account with 23-run win over DelhiChennai Super Kings (CSK) vs Delhi Capitals (DC) Highlights, IPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have opened their account with a sensational win at the Chepauk to stop their rot and move off the bottom of the table. Sanju Samson's hundred and Jamie Overton's four-wicket haul sealed the win for the side, who can look up and hope to maintain the momentum.

Catch of IPL 2026? Jansen pulls off a one-handed screamer to dismiss Ishan KishanMarco Jansen pulled off a sensational one-handed catch at the boundary to dismiss Ishan Kishan in PBKS vs SRH, swinging the momentum firmly in Punjab's favour in Mullanpur.

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
